codeReview.summary: The fix commit resolves the governed smoke-script incompatibility, adds the requested bootstrap comment, restores the 3600-second governed admin-delay default, updates the governed deployment metadata, and replaces the inflation test with a real deposit-donate-deposit scenario. But two of the six original blocking issues are still not fully closed: the new GovernanceToken mint test bypasses the actual governed deployment wiring, and the replacement reentrancy test still proves only DebtPool reentrancy, not any LendingCore entrypoint.

issues.description: The new mint coverage explicitly sidesteps the real governed deployment by deploying a fresh AccessManager + GovernanceToken pair and manually wiring the mint selector at lines 389-420. `deployGovernedSystem()` deploys the production GovernanceToken at `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deployGovernedSystem.ts:55-62`, but the helper never maps `governanceToken.mint` to `ROLE_IDS.MINTER` anywhere in its setup block (`deployGovernedSystem.ts:104-125`). As a result, this test still passes without exercising the governed deployment's actual AccessManager-gated mint path, so the prior scrutiny concern about broken or inaccessible mint wiring in the real system remains unresolved.

The replacement VAL-OZ-001 test only deploys a malicious ERC20 against a fresh `DebtPool.deposit()` path (`LendingHardened.ts:239-272`, `contracts/test/ReentrantAttacker.sol:11-43`). It never invokes any `LendingCore` entrypoint, even though the test title still claims to prove ReentrancyGuard on both LendingCore and DebtPool. This means the test would still pass if `nonReentrant` were accidentally removed from `LendingCore.depositCollateral`, `borrow`, `repay`, or `liquidate`, so the original blocking gap is only partially fixed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Solidity worker skill should document the existing `as any` workaround for Hardhat contract-factory returns in TypeScript tests. This fix run hit type errors after adding new tests and the handoff explicitly called out that guidance gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:28-50` defines the test workflow but says nothing about Hardhat typing quirks. The worker handoff at `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-16T22-13-39-254Z__governance-scrutiny-fixes__d57aca2b-7b22-4a57-a790-d7bca75277e3.json:65-70` specifically recommends adding that note after the run needed `as any` casts to clear typecheck.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not capture an important ERC-4626 nuance the worker surfaced: DebtPool relies on OZ's default `_decimalsOffset() == 0`, which makes the inflation attack net-unprofitable but still allows large donations to dilute victim redeemability materially. That implementation detail would be useful in `.factory/library/` or AGENTS guidance for future reviewers and workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/contracts/DebtPool.sol:37-204` contains no `_decimalsOffset()` override, and the fix handoff records the consequence at `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-16T22-13-39-254Z__governance-scrutiny-fixes__d57aca2b-7b22-4a57-a790-d7bca75277e3.json:58-63`. The current shared-state library listing under `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/library/` has no ERC-4626 / inflation-protection note.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, prior failed review reports, mission guidance, and the touched Solidity/TypeScript files. Result: fail. The live governed smoke path, admin-delay default, bootstrap comment, metadata fix, and inflation-attack test are in place, but the mint coverage still bypasses the real governed deployment wiring and the reentrancy coverage still does not test any LendingCore path.
